sources.issuing_organisation: Royal College of Emergency Medicine and Royal College of General Practitioners
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency and primary care; requires UAE pathway adaptation
sources.population: Adult and pediatric patients being discharged directly from an emergency department.
sources.clinical_setting: Emergency-department discharge communication with patients and primary care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for recording the clinician-confirmed outcome, treatment plan, information communicated, required follow-up, responsible party, discharge destination and safety-net discussion. It does not make the discharge decision or generate advice.

exact_sections.heading: Summary and Patient Communication
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the clinician-confirmed diagnosis or outcome and plan, patient understanding, specifically required follow-up and safety-net communication.

exact_sections.heading: GP Communication and example discharge-letter headings
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.

archive.title: Discharge to General Practice
archive.publisher: Royal College of Emergency Medicine and Royal College of General Practitioners
archive.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency and primary care; requires UAE pathway adaptation
archive.population: Adult and pediatric patients being discharged directly from an emergency department.
archive.setting: Emergency-department discharge communication with patients and primary care.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Summary and Patient Communication
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the clinician-confirmed diagnosis or outcome and plan, patient understanding, specifically required follow-up and safety-net communication.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: GP Communication and example discharge-letter headings
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.

sources.issuing_organisation: World Health Organization and International Committee of the Red Cross
sources.exact_document_title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
sources.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
sources.clinical_setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: SAMPLE history and emergency disposition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Trauma secondary survey
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.

exact_sections.heading: Extremity trauma and perfusion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Open wound and laceration assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports laceration or abrasion, location, size and depth, bleeding, contamination, foreign-body concern, underlying structures, distal perfusion, dressing, splinting, tetanus, and return or disposition documentation.
